name: Sans Souci Beach (Queen's Beach)
tagline: Waikiki's secret reef — locals snorkel here while tourists stay on the sand a mile west.
parking: Free street parking on Kalakaua Ave or paid lot at Kapiolani Park; metered spots fill early
bestTime: Early morning for calmest water and best visibility; year-round spot but summer is ideal
talkStory: This is the beach at the foot of the Natatorium — where Waikiki locals actually swim. The reef starts about 30 yards out and runs parallel to shore with solid coral and plenty of reef fish. Entry is sandy and gradual, making it one of the easiest snorkel entries in town. The sea wall of the old Natatorium creates a calm channel on the right side. Watch for sea urchins in the shallower reef sections.
